energy_model.P_ref_note: Reference CPU power draw in watts, representative of a typical desktop/server-grade CPU under load. Used as a fixed baseline across all calculations for relative comparison purposes.
energy_model.t_ref_note: Reference execution time per unit of input (n) before applying complexity scaling. Final t_ref = t_ref_base_seconds * n^(complexity_exponent implied by detected pattern).
energy_model.baseline_note: C_factor used for code with zero detected anti-patterns, representing the energy-efficient baseline for the same function size.

carbon_model.emission_factor_source: Department of Alternative Energy Development and Efficiency (DEDE), Thailand / Thailand Greenhouse Gas Management Organization (TGO)
carbon_model.emission_factor_note: Public reference value representing Thailand's national grid electricity emission factor. Used to convert estimated energy consumption into estimated CO2 equivalent.

structural_score.deduction_rule: Deduct points per detected pattern using the pattern's weight_structural value from anti_patterns.json, capped at 0 minimum.

energy_score.calculation: Compare estimated energy (kWh/year) against baseline energy (kWh/year, baseline_multiplier=1.0) for the same n and runs_per_day. energy_score = max(0, 100 - ((E_actual - E_baseline) / E_baseline) * 100), capped at 0 minimum and 100 maximum.
